meta.name: PlugDash
meta.description: Plugin catalog for EmDash - plugins, docs, and launch post.
meta.author: Abhinav

collections.label: Plugins
collections.description: The PlugDash catalog - one entry per shipped plugin.

collections.label: Docs
collections.description: Metadata for the docs pages. Bodies remain in code - this drives titles, ordering, and summaries.

collections.label: Blog
collections.description: Long-form posts. Body is Portable Text, fully editable in admin.

data.name: readtime
data.tagline: Readers decide in three seconds. Give them a reason to stay.
data.headline: Tell readers what they're committing to.
data.sub: Estimates reading time from your post's word count and shows it wherever you want. Works on every published post, automatically. Nothing to configure to ship something good.
data.home_card_copy: Readers decide in three seconds. Give them a reason to stay.
data.pairs_note: readers who finish a post are your most likely to share or heart it. Show them the path.
data.demo_caption: 5 min read - 1,240 words, calculated at publish, shown instantly.
data.setup_note: After this, every published post shows reading time. If a post doesn't have it yet, publish it again - readtime runs on every publish.

data.name: callout
data.tagline: The block every technical writer reaches for that almost no theme ships.
data.headline: The block your readers stop and read.
data.sub: Adds a Portable Text block type for info, warning, tip, and danger callouts. Shows up in the EmDash editor as a native block. Ships Callout.astro with default styles that look intentional - not like an afterthought. CSS custom properties for every visual value.
data.home_card_copy: The block every technical writer reaches for that almost no theme ships.
data.pairs_note: callouts and code blocks are the two blocks every technical writer reaches for.
data.demo_caption: info, warning, tip, danger - all four, out of the box.
data.setup_note: After this, the callout block appears in your EmDash editor. Add the component to your layout once - every post that uses callouts renders them correctly.

data.name: sharepost
data.tagline: Readers who want to share your work shouldn't have to work for it.
data.headline: Make sharing the obvious next step.
data.sub: Generates correct share URLs for Twitter/X, LinkedIn, WhatsApp, Bluesky, and email on every publish. Renders as circular icon buttons - no JavaScript libraries, no tracking scripts, no 200KB payloads. Four variants. Looks right on day one.
data.home_card_copy: Readers who want to share your work shouldn't have to work for it.
data.pairs_note: share and heart are two different signals. Most readers do one or the other. Give them both.
data.demo_caption: Circular by default. No configuration. Works in dark and light themes.
data.setup_note: After this, every published post has share URLs ready. Add the component once - every new post picks it up automatically.

data.name: heartpost
data.tagline: The simplest signal readers can send. Show them you counted it.
data.headline: The lightest form of applause.
data.sub: A heart counter backed by Cloudflare KV. One click, one count, no account needed. Ships HeartButton.astro with four variants and full CSS customisation.
data.home_card_copy: The lightest form of applause. One click, no account.
data.pairs_note: a heart and a share are different signals. Give readers both.
data.demo_caption: One click. Optimistic update. KV-backed counter.
data.setup_note: After this, every post can show a heart count. The count is stored in KV and updates without a page refresh.

data.name: shortlink
data.tagline: A short URL for every post, ready to paste anywhere.
data.headline: Every post, one clean URL to copy.
data.sub: Auto-generates short URLs for published posts, stored in Cloudflare KV. Ships CopyLink.astro with three variants - circle, pill, inline. Green check on copy, resets in two seconds.
data.home_card_copy: Every post deserves a clean URL. Copy and share in one tap.
data.pairs_note: copy link sits naturally alongside share and heart. Most readers use one of the three.
data.demo_caption: Click to copy. Green check. Resets after two seconds.
data.setup_note: After this, each published post gets a short URL automatically. The component copies it to the clipboard on click.

data.name: tocgen
data.tagline: Long posts deserve navigation. Your readers will find what they came for.
data.headline: Long posts shouldn't make readers work to find what they need.
data.sub: Parses headings from your Portable Text content and writes a nested navigation structure on every publish. Ships TableOfContents.astro with a sticky sidebar variant that follows readers as they scroll. Works out of the box. Skips short posts automatically.
data.home_card_copy: Long posts deserve navigation. Your readers will find what they came for.
data.pairs_note: readers who can see the structure and the time commitment are more likely to start.
data.demo_caption: Sticky sidebar on desktop. Nested h2 and h3. Active state as you scroll.
data.setup_note: After this, every post with three or more headings gets a table of contents. Short posts are skipped - you don't need to configure the threshold.

data.name: engage
data.tagline: Heart, share, copy - the engagement bundle for EmDash.
data.headline: The engagement bar your blog needed from day one.
data.sub: A convenience bundle that composes heartpost, sharepost, and shortlink into one component. Drop EngagementBar.astro into your Post layout and readers get all three signals with one import.
data.home_card_copy: Heart, share, copy. One import, one bar, one well-considered default.
data.pairs_note: engage wraps these three. Install all three, then use engage's component.
data.demo_caption: Heart, share buttons, copy link - in one component.
data.setup_note: engage is a bundle. Register heartpost, sharepost, and shortlink as plugins, then import EngagementBar.astro from @plugdash/engage in your Post layout.

data.name: autobuild
data.tagline: Publish in the admin. Live in 60 seconds.
data.headline: Publish in the admin. Live in 60 seconds.
data.sub: Fires your Cloudflare Pages, Netlify, or Vercel build hook on every publish. No git push. No manual redeploy. The admin becomes the thing that moves the site.
data.home_card_copy: The plugin that makes EmDash feel live.
data.pairs_note: these write metadata on publish. autobuild makes sure the live site sees the new metadata within a minute.
data.demo_caption: Publish, build hook fires, deploy kicks off, live. Four lines of config.
data.setup_note: Get your build hook URL from Cloudflare Pages, Netlify, or Vercel. Paste it as an env var. After this, every publish rebuilds the site. Debounces 5 seconds so a batch of publishes triggers one deploy, not ten.

data.title: Getting started
data.summary: Spin up a plugdash-ready EmDash site in under ten minutes.

data.title: What is EmDash
data.summary: The CMS plugdash plugs into. What it is, what it is not.

data.title: Installing plugins
data.summary: Add a plugin, register it, drop the component into your layout.

data.title: The PlugDash launch: eight plugins, one live site
data.description: Announcing PlugDash: eight MIT-licensed plugins for EmDash, all installed on the catalog site itself.

children.text: Today PlugDash ships with eight plugins for EmDash. All MIT, all on npm, all installed on this site. If a plugin works here, it works for you.

PlugDash is a plugin catalog for EmDash, the open-source CMS built on Astro and Cloudflare. Each plugin in the catalog solves a small, specific problem your blog has on day one - reading time, share buttons, a heart counter, short links, a table of contents, callouts, and publish-triggered rebuilds.

Every plugin ships with a companion Astro component. You install the plugin, add one import to your layout, and your readers see the result. No configuration needed to ship something good. Full CSS token customisation when you want to change the look.
generates share URLs for X, LinkedIn, WhatsApp, Bluesky, and email.
gives every post a short URL.
parses headings and writes a nested table of contents.
adds info, warning, tip, and danger blocks to the Portable Text editor.
bundles heart, share, and copy into one component.
fires a deploy hook on publish so the admin becomes the thing that moves the site.
Why the site runs on EmDash

A plugin catalog that does not use its own plugins is not a catalog. It is a brochure. plugdash.dev is an EmDash install with all eight plugins registered and active. The reading time on this post, the engagement bar at the bottom, the table of contents sidebar on desktop, the callout blocks - all of them are live plugins running on this page. The site is the proof.
The next batch focuses on migration and interop.
lets you move a Substack archive into EmDash in one step.
export your EmDash content back out - the anti-lock-in pair. After that:
Ship something small. Ship it open. Ship it running on itself.
